text: First of all, we explored location of the lung mass.
Apparently, we can see the lodeal in the right lower lobe of lung.
To get preliminary pathological diagnosis of the lodeal, we performed pulmonary wedge resection.
Given that the pathological diagnosis of the lodeal determines further surgical approach, we made an intraoperative frozen pathological diagnosis.
For the diagnosis of adenocarcinoma, we then performed lobectomy.
In order to more accurately determine the direction of inferior pulmonary vein the capsule around the vessel should be cleaned thoroughly.
Interestingly, variation exists in the inferior pulmonary vein of this patient.
The diagram shows intermediate and inferior pulmonary vein converged as a trunk.
Therefore, during the operation, be careful not to accidentally damage the pulmonary vein.
We cut off the inferior pulmonary vein We can see many lymph nodes near the lower lobe branches
We cleaned 11th group of lymph nodes and pathological examination proved tumor metastasis
We removed tissues around the lower lobe branches fully exposing the lower lobe branches There are many lymph nodes and this surgery was difficult
We clipped lower lobe branches while expanding the lung to verify the shape of lower lobe branches.
After conformation, we cut off lower lobe branches.
For hypoplastic lobe features, we should first conform the tunnel and then disconcrete it.
We removed lymph nodes around the lobo fissures.
The diagram shows intermediate pulmonary artery, dorsal artery and so on.
And we cut off dorsal artery and basal stem artery once.
We removed the subcarrier node lymph nodes We first cleaned its rear edge along the esophagus and then removed all lymph nodes near the subcarrier node area
We cleaned the second and fourth group lymph nodes.
We should pay attention to protect vagus nerve from injury.
We also cleaned the small inner lymph nodes
We cleaned the third group lymph nodes.
Finally, we covered the wound by hemostatic gauze in order to stop bleeding and reduce the lymph aggravation.
